text:
University College Hospital 

National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery 

Clinic Date: 02 October 2025 

Dr Susan  
University College Hospital  
National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery, London WC1N 3BG  

Dear Dr Susan,  

Daniel Morton, DOB: 22-07-1984, Hospital No: H784233 NHS No. 9462153702  
Flat 4, 29 Kingfisher Road, London, NW1 8QP 

Summary/ Diagnoses: Epilepsy with prior head injury in adolescence/low mood symptoms under primary care review/mild vitamin D insufficiency/ongoing tobacco use. 

Medication: Levetiracetam 750 mg twice daily.  
Lamotrigine 100 mg twice daily.  
Cholecalciferol 1,000 IU daily.  
Sertraline 50 mg each morning.  

I reviewed Daniel Morton in clinic today, accompanied by his partner, who helps coordinate appointments. He has been keeping a detailed paper diary for the last six months. He notes that, in the week preceding some events, there is a consistent prodrome of mood change with irritability and heightened startle, which he and his partner now use as an early warning sign. The diary has proven useful in pacing activities and avoiding sleep deprivation on those weeks. He denies recent head injuries or intercurrent illness. Appetite is stable and weight unchanged. He continues to work part‑time in a warehouse role and has not driven. 

Seizures: Based on diary entries and corroboration from his partner, he continues to experience ≤ 7 to 8 seizures per month, with month‑to‑month variation and occasional clusters around periods of poor sleep or increased stress at work. Some, but not all, events are preceded by the reported prodromal mood/irritability. There have been no prolonged episodes requiring emergency care, no injuries, and no tongue‑biting or incontinence reported in the last three months. Morning doses are generally taken on time; he missed two evening doses in the past month due to late shifts. 

Examination: Alert and orientated, speech fluent. Cranial nerves intact. No focal motor deficits. Gait steady. Cardiovascular and respiratory examinations unremarkable. 

Investigations: Routine bloods from August 2025 showed normal FBC, U&E, LFTs; vitamin D 46 nmol/L. ECG in September 2025 was normal sinus rhythm. No new neuroimaging since 2023. 

Plan: 
- No change to current anti‑seizure medication regimen today. Reinforced adherence, particularly evening doses on late‑shift days, and he will set paired alarms.  
- Continue diary tracking of prodromal mood/irritability; he will bring a copy to the next review.  
- Sleep hygiene discussed; he will aim for consistent bedtimes on non‑shift days.  
- Recheck vitamin D level in three months via the practice.  
- Safety: continues not to drive; standard seizure precautions reiterated.  
- Follow‑up in neurology clinic in six months, sooner if there is any increase beyond the current pattern of ≤ 7 to 8 seizures per month or if events change in character.  

Yours sincerely

entities.text: he continues to experience ≤ 7 to 8 seizures per month

check__Seizure Frequency Number.analysis: The letter explicitly states: "he continues to experience ≤ 7 to 8 seizures per month". This provides a numeric range per month with a leading '≤'. The instruction is to prioritize explicit numeric expressions and normalize to allowed formats. '≤ 7 to 8 per month' effectively indicates a maximum around 7 to 8 monthly; the clearest normalization within allowed formats is the stated range "7 to 8 per month". There are no other conflicting frequencies. He is not seizure free.
seizure_frequency_number: he continues to experience ≤ 7 to 8 seizures per month
